ks1.objective: Listen carefully, solve simple maths problems and organise yourself quickly into groups.

lks2.objective: Develop awareness, communication and quick thinking through football-themed maths challenges.

uks2.objective: Combine movement, decision-making and teamwork whilst solving increasingly challenging maths questions.

space.description: Create a safe playing area where players can move freely whilst dribbling their footballs.

value.name: Responsibility
value.text: Responsibility means thinking carefully, making good decisions and helping others succeed. Players should take ownership of finding the correct answer and working positively with teammates.

steps: Each player starts with a football inside the playing area.
Players dribble freely around the area.
The coach calls out a maths question.
Players work out the answer and quickly form groups of that size.
Players without a group complete a challenge before rejoining.
Restart with dribbling before the next question.
progressions: Increase the difficulty of the maths questions.
Require players to perform a ball mastery skill before grouping.
Add time limits to increase pressure.
questions: How did you work out the answer?
How did you find your group quickly?
What helped your team organise itself?

step.space: Increase space for younger children or reduce space to challenge awareness.
step.task: Use more difficult maths questions or football challenges.
step.equipment: Introduce coloured cones and require groups to move to a matching colour.
step.people: Pair confident mathematicians with less confident children to encourage support.

base.showmanship: Use football-themed maths questions, countdowns and bonus rounds. Celebrate quick thinking and teamwork as much as correct answers.
